steps.title: Fit two points from the trend
steps.content: The scatterplot has a clear downward trend. Two easy trend points are about $(0,6)$ and $(5,1)$. Use those two points to estimate the line.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: Using $(0,6)$ and $(5,1)$, the slope is $$m=\frac{1-6}{5-0}=-1$$ The intercept is $6$, so the trend line is $y=-x+6$. That has negative slope and positive $y$-intercept, matching the scatterplot.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice B has the right slope but the wrong intercept. Choices C and D have positive slope, which is the wrong direction. Choice A, $y=-x+6$, is correct.
